nf:register.nf:name: cmd
nf:register.nf:description: Command register. [1:0] fifo_mode : 00=IDLE/passthrough 01=PROC 10=FIFO_OUT 11=FIFO_IN [2] fifo_reset : pulse to reset FIFO state machine [3] arm_start : rising-edge triggers ARM CPU execution [4] gpu_start : rising-edge triggers GPU (BFloat16 tensor core) execution [5] gpu2_start : rising-edge triggers GPU2 / Simple CPU execution

nf:register.nf:name: proc_addr
nf:register.nf:description: Unified address register for host access. Used as byte address for Feature BRAM, Weight BRAM, ARM IMEM, ARM DMEM, and GPU IMEM. Dual-BRAM Routing : Bit [10] determines target: 0 = Feature BRAM (Base 0x000), 1 = Weight BRAM (Base 0x400) Feature/Weight BRAM: bits [9:2] map to the host word index ARM IMEM : bits [10:2] = 32-bit word index (rdata_sel[3]=0) ARM DMEM : bits [9:2] = 32-bit word index (rdata_sel[3]=1) GPU IMEM : bits [6:2] = 32-bit word index (rdata_sel[4]=0)

nf:register.nf:name: wdata_hi
nf:register.nf:description: Write data high word: data[63:32]. Used for 64-bit Feature/Weight BRAM writes (paired with wdata_lo). For 32-bit IMEM/DMEM programming, wdata_lo carries the word.

nf:register.nf:name: wdata_lo
nf:register.nf:description: Write data low word: data[31:0]. For Feature/Weight BRAM: lower 32 bits of 64-bit payload. For ARM/GPU IMEM and ARM DMEM programming: the 32-bit instruction or data word.

nf:register.nf:name: wdata_ctrl
nf:register.nf:description: Write control and trigger. [7:0] ctrl_byte : Feature/Weight BRAM ctrl[7:0] (EOP/SOP packet markers). Writing any new value triggers a host_we pulse to the targeted BRAM when fifo_mode=PROC and no processor is running. Upper bits [31:8] reserved.

nf:register.nf:name: rdata_sel
nf:register.nf:description:
Read/write target selector and programming interface control. [0] target : 0=Feature/Weight BRAM (default) 1=GPU2 data memory [1] arm_prog_en : 1=hold ARM in reset / programming mode [2] gpu_prog_en : 1=hold GPU in reset / programming mode [3] arm_dmem_sel: 0=ARM IMEM target 1=ARM DMEM target [4] gpu_dmem_sel: 0=GPU IMEM target 1=shared Feature/Weight BRAM [5] arm_prog_we : rising-edge pulse writes proc_addr+wdata_lo to ARM IMEM or DMEM [6] gpu_prog_we : rising-edge pulse writes proc_addr+wdata_lo to GPU IMEM [31:7] reserved.
Programming workflow (mirrors Lab 6 load_and_test.sh): ARM IMEM write : rdata_sel[1]=1 rdata_sel[3]=0, set proc_addr+wdata_lo, pulse rdata_sel[5] ARM DMEM write : rdata_sel[1]=1 rdata_sel[3]=1, set proc_addr+wdata_lo, pulse rdata_sel[5] GPU IMEM write : rdata_sel[2]=1 rdata_sel[4]=0, set proc_addr+wdata_lo, pulse rdata_sel[6] GPU data (Dual-BRAM): fifo_mode=PROC, use proc_addr + wdata_hi/lo/ctrl path (Bit 10 selects Feature vs Weight)

nf:register.nf:name: status
nf:register.nf:description: FIFO status. [1:0] fifo_mode : current mode [2] pkt_ready : 1=one complete packet buffered [15:8] pkt_len : number of 72-bit words in packet

nf:register.nf:name: rdata_hi
nf:register.nf:description: Read data high: data[63:32] from Feature BRAM, Weight BRAM, or GPU2 dmem at proc_addr. Read twice to account for synchronous BRAM read latency.

nf:register.nf:name: rdata_lo
nf:register.nf:description: Read data low: data[31:0] from Feature BRAM, Weight BRAM, or GPU2 dmem at proc_addr.

nf:register.nf:name: rdata_ctrl
nf:register.nf:description: Read ctrl: {24b0, ctrl[7:0]} from Feature BRAM or Weight BRAM at proc_addr.

nf:register.nf:name: pointers
nf:register.nf:description: Processor status and FIFO pointers. [0] arm_done : ARM halted (all threads at halt PC) [1] arm_run : ARM executing [2] gpu_done : GPU (BFloat16) completed [3] gpu_run : GPU executing [4] gpu2_done : GPU2/Simple CPU completed [5] gpu2_run : GPU2/Simple CPU executing [15:8] tail_addr : FIFO BRAM tail pointer [23:16] head_addr : FIFO BRAM head pointer
